title: catevala retusa
intro: Catevala retusa, also known as the Desert Marigold or Baileya multiradiata, is a flowering plant native to the Southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Catevala retusa include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Catevala retusa can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote healing and overall well-being.
